title: My Mother was There
description: Adachi returns from the bath to find the room dark and Shimamura pretending to sleep. A quiet, intimate moment where she considers kissing her—and sings a lullaby she didn't know she remembered.
content:
When I came back from the bath, the room was completely dark. It was still too early for lights-out, and I paused at the threshold, uneasy.

'…Shimamura?'

No answer.

A sliver of light from the hallway slipped past me as I opened the door, just enough to make out the shape of the futon already laid out on the floor—and Shimamura's legs sticking out from beneath it. Had she really fallen asleep already?

Assuming she had, I eased inside and shut the door behind me. The darkness settled in around me, close and airless, as if the night had been sealed into the room. Careful not to make a sound, I stepped closer and crouched at her side. My damp hair went forgotten as I waited for my eyes to adjust.

Gradually, her face emerged from the dark.

…She's awake. Probably.

After watching her day in and day out, I felt sure she was only pretending. Still, I couldn't say for certain.

And if she really were asleep… then what?

I hovered there, unsure, my breath catching as I studied her face. My gaze drifted to her lips, and I quickly shook my head, as if I could scatter the thought before it settled.

It wasn't because of the darkness. Or maybe it was. Either way, I kept shaking my head until the stray thoughts broke apart. A few droplets flicked from my hair, and I finally steadied myself.

It's not like I'd do anything… even if she were asleep.

…Do I want to kiss Shimamura?

The question surfaced again—the same one that had been keeping me awake night after night, winding itself tight in my chest. I turned it over, just as I always did.

And, as always, I came to the same answer. It left a faint tingling across my skin, like I'd already stepped over that line.

What would happen if I did? What would I see on the other side?

Even as the thought tugged at me, I told myself not now. Not yet.

Trusting my sense that she was only pretending, I thought about how to reach her—how to touch something deeper, something certain.

What I settled on was a lullaby.

Part of me wanted to trade places with her, too—to be the one who played the 'mother' between us for once. I wondered what that would feel like.

Was it really so nice, being someone's mother?

…I wouldn't know. I don't really understand what that means.

The song came to me without warning. At first my voice caught, rough and unsteady, but it soon smoothed out, the melody flowing on its own. I didn't stumble over the words, though I wasn't even sure what language they belonged to. It felt strange, how naturally it came. As if it had always been there, waiting.

Even when I closed my eyes and kept singing, I couldn't trace where it had come from.

Maybe, long ago—so long ago that memory itself couldn't reach it—someone had sung it to me.

There was only one person that could have been.

'That's not a lullaby I've ever heard.'

In the end, Shimamura gave herself away.

So she had been pretending.

The tension drained out of me all at once, the choice I'd been avoiding slipping quietly out of reach.

Ah… thank goodness.

'I knew you were awake.'
